id: space:live coverage spacex to launch 119 payloads on smallsat ride:2026-03-30T02:30:28.000Z
title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch 119 payloads on smallsat rideshare mission from California
author: Will Robinson-Smith
summary_long: SpaceX is gearing up to launch its third largest smallsat rideshare mission on Monday morning. The Transporter-16 mission will fly 119 payloads to a Sun-synchronous, low Earth orbit on a Falcon 9 rocket launching from Vandenberg Space Force Base. Liftoff from Space Launch Complex 4 East is scheduled for 4:02 a.m. PDT (7:02 a.m. Source: spaceflightnow.com. Date: 2026-03-30. id: consumer:blueskys next product is an ai assistant that helps build cu:2026-03-29T16:31:40.000Z
title: Bluesky's next product is an AI assistant that helps build custom social media feeds
author: Jackson Chen
summary_long: Bluesky is the latest social media platform to throw its hat into the AI chatbot ring. Bluesky, but specifically its chief innovation officer Jay Graber and her new Exploration team, built a new AI assistant called Attie that's designed to help users create custom feeds. Graber called Attie an "agentic social app" that's built on its its open-source framework called the AT Protocol. To use Attie, users can punch in prompts in natural language to generate social feeds without having to know how to code. Source: www.engadget.com. Date: 2026-03-29. Why it matters: outline likely impact on technology, business, or society. What’s next: note near-term developments or signals to watch.

title: Polygraphs have major flaws. Are there better options?
author: Sarah Scoles, Undark Magazine
summary_long: When George W. Maschke applied to work for the FBI in 1994, he had already held a security clearance for over 11 years. id: consumer:bethesda is shutting down the elder scrolls blades on june 3:2026-03-29T19:10:16.000Z
title: Bethesda is shutting down The Elder Scrolls: Blades on June 30
author: Jackson Chen
summary_long: It's a sad day for the dozens of players still grinding The Elder Scrolls: Blades. Bethesda announced that it's permanently shutting down the servers for its free-to-play mobile spinoff on June 30. First spotted on Reddit, The Elder Scrolls: Blades has already been delisted from the App Store and Google Play store, and is currently unavailable on the Nintendo Store. In the meantime, players will receive a free bundle of Gems and Sigils, while all items in the in-game store are available for just one Gem or Sigil each. Source: www.engadget.com. Date: 2026-03-29. Why it matters: outline likely impact on technology, business, or society. What’s next: note near-term developments or signals to watch.

id: nextfrontiers:why ai still cant beat a new video game:2026-03-29T17:00:06.000Z
title: Why AI still can't beat a new video game
summary_long: For decades, video games have served as a proving ground for artificial intelligence. From early checkers programs to systems that conquered chess and Go, each milestone has seemed to bring machines closer to human-like intelligence. But a new paper by Julian Togelius and colleagues argues that this narrative is misleading. Despite impressive victories, today's AI still struggles with a deceptively simple challenge: playing a game it has never seen before. Source: techxplore.com. Date: 2026-03-29. Why it matters: outline likely impact on technology, business, or society. What’s next: note near-term developments or signals to watch.

id: nextfrontiers:ai benchmark helps robots plan and complete their chores in :2026-03-29T15:30:02.000Z
title: AI benchmark helps robots plan and complete their chores in the real world
summary_long: No matter how sophisticated they are, robots can often be indecisive and struggle with multi-step chores in the real world. For example, if you tell a robot to tidy a messy room, it might understand the goal but not know where to grab each object. It could even end up inventing steps. To address these common mistakes, Microsoft and a group of academics have developed an AI benchmark system to improve the accuracy of robot planning. Source: techxplore.com. Date: 2026-03-29. Why it matters: outline likely impact on technology, business, or society. What’s next: note near-term developments or signals to watch.
